Code fragment: public final TableField<MealsRecord, Float> PRICE = createField(DSL.name("Price"), SQLDataType.REAL.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The PRICE column uses SQLDataType.REAL (a floating-point type) which can cause rounding errors when storing monetary values. Use SQLDataType.DECIMAL or SQLDataType.NUMERIC with appropriate precision and scale (e.g., DECIMAL(10,2)) to ensure accurate price calculations.

Code fragment: public final TableField<ReviewRecord, Integer> MEAL_ID = createField(DSL.name("meal_id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The MEAL_ID column references the Meals table but lacks a foreign key constraint, which could lead to orphaned records or invalid meal references. Add a foreign key constraint referencing MEALS.MEAL_ID to ensure referential integrity.

Code fragment: public final TableField<ReviewRecord, LocalDateTime> CREATED_AT = createField(DSL.name("created_at"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME(0).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME)), this, "");
Explanation: The CREATED_AT column has a default value but is still marked as NULLABLE, which is inconsistent since it will always have a value in practice. Mark the column as NOT NULL to accurately reflect its intended usage.

Code fragment: public final TableField<UsersRecord, Integer> FAV_MEAL = createField(DSL.name("fav_meal"), SQLDataType.INTEGER, this, "");
Explanation: The FAV_MEAL column appears to reference a meal but lacks a foreign key constraint to the Meals table. Add a foreign key constraint referencing MEALS.MEAL_ID to ensure referential integrity.

Code fragment: public final TableField<UsersRecord, String> DONT_SHOW_MEAL = createField(DSL.name("dont_show_meal"), SQLDataType.CLOB.defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("''"), SQLDataType.CLOB)), this, "");
Explanation: The DONT_SHOW_MEAL column uses an empty string as a default value to represent missing data instead of NULL. Use NULL to represent missing values, as empty strings can be confused with intentional empty values and complicate queries.
